title: Filesystem other than VFS when running Podman in a Docker container on Kubernetes
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

We would like to use Podman for builds on our enterprise Jenkins installation which runs on top of Kubernetes v1.16 EKS and uses Docker as the underlying containerization platform. VFS performance however is suboptimal (as expected.)  Is it possible to get overlay working in this scenario? When we try to use overlay we encounter the following error:

```
Error: error mounting new container: error mounting build container "7c8ab2bfab6bd5b9670c7b6b2cb7c954b454ddb6490774bd9628d457a7fd5faa": error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/fe81a50663cd85853c9974e5a58fd537c198ff081779332548807bd70217e301/merged: using mount program /usr/local/bin/fuse-overlayfs: fuse: device not found, try 'modprobe fuse' first
fuse-overlayfs: cannot mount: No such file or directory
: exit status 1
```

We are running in an unprivileged container, but do give SYS_ADMIN and SYS_RESOURCE.

```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: ***
spec:
  containers:
  - name: podman
    image: ***
    tty: true
    command: [ "cat" ]
    imagePullPolicy: Always
    volumeMounts:
      - name: shared-build-output
        mountPath: /var/run/outputs
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        add: [ "SYS_ADMIN", "SYS_RESOURCE" ]
```

comments.body: I believe this should be possible, but you'll need to pass `/dev/fuse` from the host into the container. @rhatdan would know more, though.

Yes you need /dev/fuse mounted into the container. If you are using cri-o you can just setup cri-o to always mount /dev/fuse into containers, and problem is solved.  In k8s you can volume mount them in.  Sadly currently there is no equivalent to podman --device /dev/fuse in k8s.

@giuseppe @mrunalp Thoughts.

For those not on CRI-O i.e., EKS and trying to use Podman with overlay, the fuse-device-plugin is a viable alternative. Like CRI-O, you can automount /dev/fuse. In the fuse-device-plugin case it's a matter of adding the appropriate configuration in your pod YAML.

Steps required follow:

1. EKS Kernel upgrade (required for fuse-overlay to work):
 
sudo amazon-linux-extras install kernel-ng
 
2. Load fuse device driver (should set this to load on boot):
 
modprobe fuse
 
3. Enable kubelet feature gate by modifying the DAEMON_ARGS in the environment file used by /usr/lib/systemd/system/kubelet.service:
 
--feature-gates=DevicePlugins=true
 
4. Deploy the fuse-device-plugin daemonset using kubectl:
 
Daemonset: https://github.com/kuberenetes-learning-group/fuse-device-plugin/blob/master/fuse-device-plugin-k8s-1.16.yml
 
5. Modify your pod YAML to specify fuse device resource:
 
  containers:
    - name: podman
      image:  docker.mycompany.com/podman:latest
      tty: true
      command: [ "cat" ]
      imagePullPolicy: Always
      securityContext:
        capabilities:
          add: [ "SYS_ADMIN", "SYS_RESOURCE" ]
resources:
        limits:
          github.com/fuse: 1
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

We have just published

https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-container
And
https://www.redhat.com/sysadmin/podman-inside-kubernetes

Please read these and see if they help solve your problem.  Reopen if you need more information.
